section: Chapter: The Wit That Won Hearts

questions.question: Who was Tenali Rama?
questions.correct_answer: A witty poet and advisor in the court of King Krishnadeva Raya
questions.explanation: Tenali Rama was famous for his intelligence, humour, and clever problem-solving in the Vijayanagara court.

questions.question: Which empire was ruled by King Krishnadeva Raya?
questions.explanation: Krishnadeva Raya was one of the most famous rulers of the Vijayanagara Empire, known for supporting art and literature.

questions.question: Why did the king become angry with the queen?
questions.correct_answer: Because she yawned while he was reciting his poem
questions.explanation: The king thought the queen was disrespecting his poetry when she yawned, though she was only tired.

questions.question: Who did the queen seek help from to solve the problem?
questions.explanation: The queen believed that Tenali Rama’s wisdom and wit could help resolve the misunderstanding with the king.

questions.question: What clever idea did Tenali Rama present in the court?
questions.correct_answer: He spoke about special paddy seeds that could produce greater yield
questions.explanation: Tenali Rama used the example of paddy seeds to indirectly make the king realise his mistake.

questions.question: How did Tenali Rama make the king realise his mistake?
questions.correct_answer: By comparing yawning while sowing seeds to the queen yawning while listening to the poem
questions.explanation: Rama cleverly explained that yawning is natural and not a sign of disrespect.

questions.question: What did the king realise after hearing Tenali Rama’s explanation?
questions.correct_answer: That he had misunderstood the queen
questions.explanation: The king realised that the queen’s yawning was natural and not meant to insult him.

questions.question: What did the king do after realising his mistake?
questions.explanation: The king admitted that his pride had blinded him and asked the queen for forgiveness.

questions.question: Why were witty advisors valued in royal courts?
questions.correct_answer: Because they could solve problems wisely and creatively
questions.explanation: Witty advisors like Tenali Rama helped rulers make better decisions and resolve conflicts peacefully.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.correct_answer: Wisdom and humour can solve conflicts effectively
questions.explanation: The story shows that clever thinking and understanding others can resolve misunderstandings.

notes.title: Tenali Rama
notes.content: Tenali Rama was a famous poet and advisor known for his wit and clever solutions in the Vijayanagara court.

notes.title: Vijayanagara Empire
notes.content: The Vijayanagara Empire flourished under King Krishnadeva Raya, who supported literature, art, and learning.

notes.title: Misunderstanding in the Story
notes.content: The king misunderstood the queen’s yawn as disrespect, which caused a quarrel between them.

notes.title: Tenali Rama’s Strategy
notes.content: Tenali Rama cleverly used the example of sowing paddy seeds to help the king understand that yawning is natural.

notes.title: Resolution of the Conflict
notes.content: The king realised his mistake and apologised to the queen, restoring peace in the palace.

facts.fact: Tenali Rama was one of the famous poets in King Krishnadeva Raya’s court.
The Vijayanagara Empire was known for its cultural achievements and literary development.
Misunderstandings can arise from simple actions like yawning.
Wisdom and humour can help resolve conflicts peacefully.
